name: Apricot Chutney with Raisins & Currants
recipeIngredient: 10 large Garlic cloves - peeled & coarsely chopped
1 1-inch piece of fresh ginger - peeled & coarsely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Put the apricots in a bowl. Pour 4 cups of hot water over them and let them soak for an hour.
2. Put the garlic, ginger into the container of an electric blender or food processor along with 1/4 cup of the vinegar. Blend until smooth.
3. Empty the apricots and their soaking liquid into a heavy stainless-steel or porcelain-lined pot. Add the garlic-ginger mixture, the remaining vinegar, sugar, salt, and cayenne. Bring to a boil.
4. Simmer on a medium flame, stirring frequently, for 45 minutes. Lower heat if necessary.
5. Add the raisins and currants and cook, stirring, another half hour or until chutney takes on a thick, glazed look. (It will thicken slightly as it cools.)
6. Let the chutney cool and store, refrigerated, in lidded glass or ceramic jars.
